info.name: Simba API
info.description:
Welcome to the Pargo API documentation.

First make sure you have your login credentials. These credentials can be requested at Pargo. Please visit https://pargo.co.za for more information.

For the majority of requests a requestReference property is send back in the response. You can find this value in the meta property.
When you encounter any problems, please give us this reference and we’ll be able to lookup this specific request en see what went wrong.

In this documentation we will use curly brackets {{ and }} to indicate that these values are variables and should be replaces by your real values (without the curly brackets). Eg. when the example looks like this

{
    "property": "{{abcdef}}"
}

Your real request should be like this

{
    "property": "yourvalue"
}

item.name: Authentication Module

response.name: Authentication
Authentication Rate Limit Error
Refresh token

item.description:
We use the OAuth2 protocol for authentication.

As of right now we don’t have a need for the grant_type, client_id and client_secret part of the protocol.
Therefore the only thing you need to send us for the retrieving of a token is the username and password.

When requesting a token through an Authentication or Refresh token request you receive an access_token, refresh_token and expires_in.

To authenticate your API requests you need to add an Authentication header to all requests as follows:

Key: 'Authorization"

Value: 'Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN'

(notice the space between Bearer and the token)

The access_token expires after some time. This is indicated in the expires_in. expires_in is the amount of seconds the token is valid after creating it (now it is set to 4200 seconds or 70 minutes). When a access_token is expired you can request a new one using the Authenticatio request, but you can also use the Refresh token request. If you choose for the second option you need to send us the refresh_token you've received when requesting the access_token. If the refresh_token is still valid, you'll receive a new access_token and refresh_token. Right now the refresh_token expires in 10 days.

When developing an OAuth2 API client, it's crucial to understand that authentication should not be performed on every request but instead done once to obtain an access token that is reused for subsequent requests. Here are the reasons why:

  1. Efficiency and Performance:

    1. Minimizing Latency: Authenticating every request introduces significant overhead since each authentication call would involve extra round-trip communication with the authentication server. This would increase the latency for every API call and negatively impact the application's responsiveness.

    2. Increased Load on Authentication Server: Every request made with authentication would result in additional load on the authentication server. If many clients authenticate frequently, this could lead to server overload, slower responses, or potential downtime.

  2. Rate Limiting and Throttling:

    1. Authenticating on every request increases the number of API calls to Pargo, potentially causing the client to hit rate limits, which would result in failed requests.

    2. Repeated authentication can trigger rate-limiting measures or even temporary suspension of access for breaching usage limits.

  3. Security and Stability:

    1. Reduced Exposure: OAuth2 tokens are typically short-lived and secure. Once authenticated, the access token is used until it expires, reducing the need to repeatedly transmit sensitive credentials over the network. Authenticating on every request increases the risk of exposing credentials, especially if there's a man-in-the-middle attack.

    2. Token Reuse: After the initial authentication, OAuth2 tokens can be reused within their lifespan (typically several minutes to hours). This reuse reduces the risk of sensitive information (like the user's password) being exposed repeatedly.

  4. Token Expiration and Refresh:

    1. Access Token Expiration: OAuth2 tokens have expiration times. Instead of constantly re-authenticating, clients should handle the expiration by refreshing the token using a refresh token (which is done in the background). This allows for a more seamless user experience without additional interaction.

    2. Avoid Credential Reuse: Constantly sending credentials increases the risk of interception or leaking credentials through logs or debugging information. OAuth2 tokens, once issued, can be revoked or expire automatically, thus offering more secure session management.

  5. Simplified Code and Maintenance:

    1. Reduced Complexity: If authentication happens only once, the code can be simpler and cleaner. There’s no need to handle authentication for every single request, which makes the client easier to maintain and scale.

    2. Standard OAuth2 Flow: OAuth2 is designed to use tokens in this manner—authenticate once, then use tokens until they expire. Going against this standard by authenticating every request breaks the natural flow of the OAuth2 protocol and may introduce unnecessary complexity and confusion.

Downsides of Authenticating on Every Request:

Performance Degradation: Each authentication step introduces unnecessary delay in processing the actual request. Instead of simply sending an access token, the client has to wait for the OAuth provider to verify credentials and return a new token, slowing down the API responses.

Higher Risk of Repeated Failures: Authenticating on every request opens up more points of failure, such as network outages or temporary issues with the OAuth provider. If the provider’s authentication service is temporarily unavailable, repeated authentication will likely fail, making the API client unusable.

Wasted Resources: Each authentication request consumes additional CPU, bandwidth, and other resources, which can be a waste if an existing access token could have been reused.

Summary:
In OAuth2, the recommended approach is to authenticate once, retrieve an access token, and use that token until it expires. Once it expires, the client can refresh the token using the refresh token or re-authenticate if necessary. Constant re-authentication introduces unnecessary performance issues, security risks, and complexity, making it far from ideal.

item.name: Rate Limit
item.description:
This api uses a rate limiter. This means you'll be able to send us a maximum of 60 requests per minute. Every response will include the X-RateLimit-Limit (the total amount of requests per minute) header and the X-RateLimit-Remaining (how many requests are remaining for this minute) header. If you surpass this limit you'll receive a response with the 429 Too Many Requests status code. The body of that request is:

{
    "meta": [],
    "errors": [
        {
            "title": "Too Many Attempts.",
            "detail": "",
            "code": 0,
            "meta": []
        }
    ],
    "success": false,
    "statusCode": 429
}

item.name: Map Module
item.description:
Implementation Guidelines

  1. Your page can implement the Pargo Points page via an IFrame or by opening a new window (see code examples in page source) at: https://map.pargo.co.za/demo.html

  2. The Pargo Points page URL is the following:
    https://map.pargo.co.za/?token=uvwNZGSiUypW1GHiTGFZiqWWu2W4s78SwUIEWp10GKogqJKt

  3. An additional GET or POST argument called 'address' can be passed through, containing an address string.
    Example: https://map.pargo.co.za/?token=uvwNZGSiUypW1GHiTGFZiqWWu2W4s78SwUIEWp10GKogqJKt&address=2 Rembrand Street, Sandton, Johannesburg
    If an address request parameter is received, the Pargo page's map will open up centered on this address (if the address string can be reverse geolocated).
    If no address request parameter is received, the map will attempt to retrieve the user's current location, and will center the map there.

  4. if (window.addEventListener) { window.addEventListener("message", selectPargoPoint, false); } else { window.attachEvent("onmessage", selectPargoPoint); }

  5. Your page must also contain a javascript method called 'selectPargoPoint(item)' which is called by the Pargo Points page when a user selects a particular point.
    The 'item' parameter contains an object called 'data', which is a JSON array, containing details of the selected point.

  6. The JSON array elements of the item.data parameter are:

{
"address1": "6th Floor",
"address2": "62 Roeland Street",
"addressSms": "6th Floor 62 Roeland Street",
"businessHours": "Mon-Fri: 8am-5pm",
"city": "Cape Town",
"distance": 0.06510704065710372,
"isPublic": false,
"latitude": -33.930081910279,
"longitude": 18.422441015418,
"pargoPointCode": "pup2",
"phoneNumber": "+27214473636",
"photo": "https://example.org/a48fe6fa-87b4-4cf1-bfc1-41e1450631f8/pup_photo/large.jpg",
"photo_small": null,
"postalcode": "8001",
"province": "Western Cape",
"retailChainName": "Pargo",
"storeName": "Pargo Head Office",
"storeOwner": "Pargo",
"suburb": "Gardens"
}

7. function selectPargoPoint(item){ alert("Selected Pargo Point: " + item.data["pargoPointCode"]); }

8. You can filter both the map and list views to display only Pargo pickup points within a specified radius. To enable or adjust this setting, please contact Pargo.

9. The list of Pargo pickup points can also be ordered based on custom criteria instead of the default proximity-based sorting. To enable or adjust this setting, please contact Pargo.

Some example code:

For the following example to work, make sure there is an iframe with a src="https://map.pargo.co.za/?token=uvwNZGSiUypW1GHiTGFZiqWWu2W4s78SwUIEWp10GKogqJKt" and id="iframe". You also need a div with id="selected-pup"

if (window.addEventListener) {
    window.addEventListener("message", selectPargoPoint, false);
} else {
    window.attachEvent("onmessage", selectPargoPoint);
}
function selectPargoPoint(item){
    var html = "Selected Pargo Point Code:  " + item.data["pargoPointCode"] + "";
    html += "Address:  " + item.data["addressSms"] + "";
    let selected = document.getElementById("selected-pup");
    selected.innerHTML = html;
    return false;
}

Query Parameters Supported

parameter description example
token to use custom map based on specific client requirements
lat to focus the map on load on a specific geolocation https://map.pargo.co.za/?lat=-33.968558313704115&lng=18.59839078320973
lng to focus the map on load on a specific geolocation
address to focus the map on load on a specific location https://map.pargo.co.za/?address=62%20roeland%20street
pickup_point to focus the map on load on a specific pickup point https://map.pargo.co.za/?pickup_point=pup1000
emitExtraEvents=true to emit events for 3rd party integrations

request.description:
Use this endpoint to get the available pickup points, connected to your account

There a some optional parameters you can send us to change the response

Param Description
limit Number of items per page optional, default = 100
page Which page you want to request optional, default = 1
sort You can sort/order the results of an request with the sort parameter. The structure is 'columnname direction'. The direction can either be '+' (ASC) or '-' (DESC). Example: distance+
pickupPointCode Search for a specific pickupPointCode
country Search for a specific country code (ISO 3166-1 format)
province Search for a specific province
suburb Search for a specific suburb
city Search for a specific city
postal_code Search for a specific postal_code
name Search if the name contains this value
isPaperless Search for pickup points which can/can't ( true / false) handle paperless returns
maxDistance Return results within certain distance (in meters)
retailChainNameExact Search for a specific retail chain. Example: Clicks

You'll get a paginated response. In the meta attribute of the response you'll see the total amount, current count of data in this request, the used limit, the used offset and the current page. These values can be changed using the parameters above.

response.name: Get Pickup Points
Get Pickup Points

header.value: GET, PUT, POST, DELETE, PATCH, OPTIONS

request.url: https://api.staging.pargo.co.za/pickup_points?address=Woodlands house 17a woodlands road, woodstock, 7925, Cape Town&country=ZA&limit=5&sort=distance+
request.description:
Use this endpoint to get the available pickup points with the distance based on a geocoded address. The distance is shown in meters.

You can use the same parameters as the "default" Get Pickup Points, but for the geocoding you have to use these 2

Param Description
address Address to be geocoded required for geocoding
country Country code using the ISO 3166-1 format with 2 letters (eg. ZA, SZ, LS) required for geocoding

In addition of the pagination meta you'll also see the geocoderAccuracy and geocoderAddress. These are based on the results of the geocoding. The highest geocoderAccuracy is 5, if this number is lower that could mean we found a region instead of a specific address.

If the geocoderAccuracy is to low, you'll receive a error with title = Low accuracy and statusCode = 422. If the geocoder couldn't find your address at all, you'll receive a error with title = No geocoding results and statusCode = 422.

item.name: Autocomplete

response.name: Simple autocomplete
With Context
With Matches

item.description:
You can integrate your address forms autocomplete functionality using Pargo's Route Guide.

(Please note: we'll be adding some different way of authentication in the near future for these routes)

  • The supported address fields are:
    • province
    • suburb
    • postalCode
    • city
  • In the search field you put what you are searching for (eg. city, postalCode) and in the value field you put the actual input.
  • Optionally you can send us a context object alongside your search/value fields. When sending us the context, the system will filter out results that do not match you exact context values. This greatly improves the quality of the results.
  • The context object can contain the same values that you can search for.
  • Optionally, you can also add the with_matches url parameter. When you add this to your request, the system will also return the 5 closest total matches alongside the autocomplete values. This can be used for even quicker filling in of the address fields by the user.
    Key Description
    search The name of the field that you're currently processing
    value The current value of the field you're currently processing
    context optional object containing your context values
    context.province optional already used province
    context.suburb optional already used suburb
    context.postalCode optional already used postalCode
    context.city optional already used city

item.name: Sending as a different entity
item.description:
All users are connected to one entity (eg. one supplier or one courier). However it is possible to send a request as a different entity (for example when you're a supplier but you also want to send updates as your own courier). In consultation with Pargo, 1 or more entities can be added and set up as a sendAs. You’ll receive the references for these entities. In the request you can add a meta property at the root level where you can add the sendAs property. The contents of this field should be the reference of the entity.

Example (sending W2P order as a different supplier):

{
    "meta": {
        "sendAs": "sup12345"
    },
    "data": {
        "type": "W2P",
        "attributes": {
            "warehouseAddressCode": "Primary",
            "returnAddressCode": "Primary",
            "trackingCode": "PAR0000000000",
            "externalReference": null,
            "pickupPointCode": "pup1",
            "consignee": {
                "firstName": "Pargo",
                "lastName": "CPT",
                "email": "support@pargo.co.za",
                "phoneNumbers": ["+27214473636", "+27214473637"]
            }
        }
    }
}

Example (sending courier updates as a different courier):

{
    "meta": {
        "sendAs": "cou12345"
    },
    "data": {
        "type": "courierTrackingInfo",
        "attributes": {
            "code": 10,
            "timestamp": "2019-01-01T09:27:36+02:00",
            "orderReference": "TRACKINGCODE",
            "externalTrackingReference": "YOURREFERENCE",
            "description": "The package is registered in our system"
        }
    }
}

item.name: Quotation Module

response.name: Get Quotation W2P
Get Quotation W2D

item.description:
The quotation service, available upon request from Pargo, currently supports only W2D (Warehouse to Door) or W2P (Warehouse to Pickup Point) processes.

To obtain a quotation, submit the same information as you would when creating a new order, with a few additional required values. Specifically, you must include the attributes.totalParcels attribute and provide at least one weight measurement (either cubicWeight or deadWeight) for each parcel—even if you’re sending only one parcel.

IMPORTANT: For the most accurate quote, Pargo recommends sending both cubicWeight AND deadWeight, as the higher of the two will be used to calculate the rate. If only ONE value is provided, the system will use that value exclusively, which may result in an inaccurate quote.

When using the W2P process, the parcels array is ignored and not required, but a pickupPointCode is mandatory. Based on the customer’s address and the warehouse location, a deliveryType (main/regional/local) and the price will be provided in the response.

item.description:
A W2P order is an order that starts at a warehouse location, is being delivered at a pickup point and there collected by the customer.

Note:

  • When you want to send an order for one of your child suppliers, you can fill in the owner property. Please contact Pargo if you have child suppliers so they can set everything up. They'll also tell you what the contents of this property should be.

  • The trackingCode, externalReference, packageValue and packageDescription properties are optional. The trackingcode must include a valid prefix as discussed with Pargo.

  • If no trackingCode provided, the system will generate one for you.

  • Either the consignee.email or consignee.phoneNumbers are required (you can send both).

  • IMPORTANT: It is best practice to provide both cubicWeight and deadWeight for each order to ensure the accurate rate calculations.

  • It is possible to send us multiple phone numbers for a consignee if this person should be notified on multiple phone numbers.

  • You can send either the pickupPointCode or pickupPointExternalReference. The pickupPointCode is the reference you'll get from the map or pickup point api endpoint. For specific vendors it's possible to set up a pickupPointExternalReference for some pickup points. Please contact Pargo for this functionality.

  • It is also possible to send no pickuppoint at all. In that case Pargo will assign a pickuppoint for you. To do this, we require you to send atleast the consignee.suburb, consignee.postalCode and consignee.city values. Optional you can send consignee.address1, consignee.address2, consignee.province and consignee.country.

  • Pargo can store multiple warehouse locations for your account. One of these locations is defined as the default/fallback location. Every location has a reference. You can specify what warehouse location has to be used for that order using the warehouseAddressCode parameter. For the return address you can use the parameter returnAddressCode. These 2 parameters are optional. When not provided the system will use the default/fallback location.

  • It is possible to specify which courier should be used for a specific order. You can specify this using the courierCode parameter. In consultation with Pargo we can allow your account to send specific courier codes. If left empty, the system will use the default courier.

  • Cash on Delivery (Only for Egypt) paymentOnCollectionAmount

  • You can override the default company name, support phone number, and support email address presented to the customer. To apply custom values, specify them in the communications property.
    IMPORTANT: The values provided will be used on the Track & Trace page and in all customer notifications, including email, SMS, and WhatsApp messages. Note: this feature isn't enabled by default, so please contact Pargo if this feature is needed.

  • If you have a lot of warehouse addresses that cannot be communicated all initially you can send the entire warehouse address in the request itself. Pargo will create a warehouse entry (if it is a new one) on the fly and attach that order to it. Use the warehouseAddress and/or returnAddress properties to do so. If code is provided, the existing warehouse will be used and updated accordingly. Note: this feature isn't enabled by default, so please contact Pargo if this feature is needed.

item.description:
A W2D order is an order that starts at a warehouse location and is delivered at the customers door.

Note:

  • When you want to send an order for one of your child suppliers, you can fill in the owner property. Please contact Pargo if you have child suppliers so they can set everything up. They'll also tell you what the contents of this property should be.

  • The trackingCode, externalReference and packageDescription properties are optional. The trackingcode must include a valid prefix as discussed with Pargo.

  • If no trackingCode provided, the system will generate one for you.

  • serviceType is optional. Possible values are ECO and NXT.

  • Either the consignee.email or consignee.phoneNumbers are required (you can send both).

  • IMPORTANT: It is best practice to provide both cubicWeight and deadWeight for each order to ensure the accurate rate calculations.

  • It is possible to send us multiple phone numbers for a consignee if this person should be notified on multiple phone numbers.

  • Pargo can store multiple warehouse locations for your account. One of these locations is defined as the default/fallback location. Every location has a reference. You can specify what warehouse location has to be used for that order using the warehouseAddressCode parameter. For the return address you can use the parameter returnAddressCode. These 2 parameters are optional. When not provided the system will use the default/fallback location.

  • consignee.country has to be a valid country code using the ISO 3166-1 format with 2 letters (eg. ZA, SZ)

  • If you want to send multiple parcels/pieces you can put the total amount of parcels in the totalParcels (default = 1)

  • When you have additional details for your parcel(s), you can put them in the parcels attribute. For every parcel you can add the externalReference, cubicWeight, deadWeight, length, width and height. The order in this list is the same as the sequence for the parcels. So the first entry in parcels is the first parcel/piece in our system.

  • When sending us a multi parcel/piece order, the generated label pdf will contain individual labels for every parcel.

  • It is possible to generate an OTP code for the order which is included in the relevant webhook payload. Please contact Pargo if you would like this feature enabled.

item.description:
A P2W_RETURN is an order that starts when the customer drops the parcel at the selected pickup point and is delivered at the selected warehouse location

Note:

  • When you want to send an order for one of your child suppliers, you can fill in the owner property. Please contact Pargo if you have child suppliers so they can set everything up. They'll also tell you what the contents of this property should be.

  • The trackingCode, externalReference and packageDescription properties are optional. If provided, the trackingcode has to have a valid prefix as discussed with Pargo. If no trackingCode provided, the system will generate one for you.

  • Either the consignee.email or consignee.phoneNumbers are required (you can send both).

  • It is possible to send us multiple phone numbers for a consignee if this person should be notified on multiple phone numbers

  • Pargo can store multiple warehouse locations for your account. One of these locations is defined as the default/fallback location. Every location has a reference. You can specify what warehouse location has to be used for that order using the warehouseAddressCode parameter. This parameter is optional. When not provided the system will use the default/fallback location.

  • It is possible to override/set the company name, support phone number and support email address being shown to the customer instead of the defaults. To send these values you can use the communications property. Note: this feature isn't enabled by default, so please contact Pargo if this feature is needed.

  • If you have a lot of warehouse addresses that cannot be communicated all initially you can send the entire warehouse address in the request itself. Pargo will create a warehouse entry (if it is a new one) on the fly and attach that order to it. Use the warehouseAddress properties to do so. If warehouse code is known, the system will attempt to match by provided code and will update the existing record with new address details before creating a new warehouse entry. Note: this feature isn't enabled by default, so please contact Pargo if this feature is needed.

  • serviceType is optional. Possible values are ECO and NXT.
  • Either the consignee.email or consignee.phoneNumbers are required (you can send both).
  • It is possible to send us multiple phone numbers for a consignee if this person should be notified on multiple phone numbers.
  • Pargo can store multiple warehouse locations for your account. One of these locations is defined as the default/fallback location. Every location has a reference. You can specify what warehouse location has to be used for that order using the warehouseAddressCode parameter. For the return address you can use the parameter returnAddressCode. These 2 parameters are optional. When not provided the system will use the default/fallback location.
  • consignee.country has to be a valid country code using the ISO 3166-1 format with 2 letters (eg. ZA, SZ)
  • If you want to send multiple parcels/pieces you can put the total amount of parcels in the totalParcels (default = 1)
  • When you have additional details for your parcel(s), you can put them in the parcels attribute. For every parcel you can add the externalReference, cubicWeight, length, width and height. The order in this list is the same as the sequence for the parcels. So the first entry in parcels is the first parcel/piece in our system.
  • When sending us a multi parcel/piece order, the generated label pdf will contain individual labels for every parcel.

item.description:
A P2P order is an order that has 2 consignees, a sender and a receiver. The sender prepares the parcel and drops it off at the drop off point. From there the parcel will be transported to the collection point where the receiver can collect the parcel.

This order module should be used in conjunction with either the Map Module, or the Pickup Points Module, in order to make the pickup-points available for selection to senders and receivers.

General Notes:

  • The trackingCode, externalReference and packageDescription properties are optional.
  • If no trackingCode is provided, the system will generate one for you.
  • If you would like to generate your own trackingcode it must include a valid prefix as discussed with Pargo.
  • Either the sender.email or sender.phonenumbers are required (you can send both).
  • Either the receiver.email or receiver.phonenumbers are required (you can send both).
  • packageValue can be used to pass the monetary value of the parcel contents to Pargo, for use should an insurance claim arise. The value must be a number or null, eg. "packageValue": 500. This field is optional and can be left as null.

Advanced Notes:

  • When you want to send an order for one of your child suppliers, you can fill in the owner property. Please contact Pargo if you have child suppliers for configurations and to ensure you have clarity on the contents required for this property.

  • You can send either the dropOffPickupPointCode / collectionPickupPointCode or dropOffPickupPointExternalReference / collectionPickupPointExternalReference. The PickupPointCode is the reference you'll get from the map or pickup point api endpoint. For specific vendors it's possible to set up a PickupPointExternalReference for some pickup points. Please contact Pargo for this functionality.

This order module should be used in conjunction with either the Map Module, or the Pickup Points Module, in order to make the drop-off points available for selection by the sender.

Advanced Notes:

  • When you want to send an order for one of your child suppliers, you can fill in the owner property. Please contact Pargo if you have child suppliers for configurations and to ensure you have clarity on the contents required for this property.

  • You can send either the dropOffPickupPointCode or dropOffPickupPointExternalReference. The dropOffPickupPointCode is the reference you'll get from the map or pickup point api endpoint. For specific vendors it's possible to set up a dropOffPickupPointExternalReference for some pickup points. Please contact Pargo for this functionality.

item.name: Get Order Label

Get order label by external reference - Success

item.description:
This endpoint can be used to retrieve the order label URL if the label is generated and available for various order process types including the multi-parcel orders.

The request will be successful if label has has been successfully generated and the user is the owner or initiator of the order.

Both the order waybill number and external reference are accepted. If a # needs to be prepended for an external reference search, please contact Pargo.

It’s possible to send one or multiple orders in the same requests. When you’re sending multiple orders in a request a batch will be made and you’ll receive the batch reference in the meta property of the response.

It is possible to send us 1 order at a time or multiple in a batch. The data that has to be send for a specific order can be different depending on what type of order it is. Below you'll find examples for sending a specific order and what data is required. At the end we'll show you how to send a batch requests using one or multiple order types. Every type is the same basic structure. It has a type parameter which indicates what order type should be used and a attributes parameter. The contents in attributes depends on the selected type.

response.name: Send batch tracking info Copy

item.description:
When sending courier tracking info make sure you are authorized with the courier login credentials.

A single block of tracking info contains a code, timestamp (valid ISO8601 format), orderReference, parcelReference (optional), externalTrackingReference (optional, for your own use), description (optional), attempts (optional, if you know how many times you've tried this action for this order), isForReturn (optional), ETA (optional, valid ISO8601 format, if you know when the delivery is expected), geolocation (optional, should be within valid range and valid lat/lng values).

When sending a POD code 50/52, you can optionally add fields signature (must be a valid Base 64 Encoded string), and receiverName. There is also a support for the optional numeric value from 1 to 8 for the supplementCode when sent with the "code": 50 to specify the type of delivery in greater detail as follows:

supplementCode code
customer 1
damage 2
letterbox 3
neighbour_signed 4
nearby_store 5
neighbour 6
po_box 7
reception 8

It is also possible to send us the alternativeAddress property. This property is used when you have a code 50 (POD / Delivered) on a different address than originally expected. If the package is delivered at a pickupPoint instead of a home address, the contents of this attribute should be formatted like this:

{
    "data": {
        "type": "courierTrackingInfo",
        "attributes": {
            ...
            "alternativeAddress": {
                "pickupPointCode": "{{pup1}}"
            }
        }
    }
}

The code corresponds to one of our courier stages so we can map this request.

Code Description
10 Registered (the order is successfully registered in your system)
11 Collection request received
12 Collection scheduled
13 Collection allocated to driver
14 Collection accepted by driver
19 Collection completed
20 Collected (the order is physically collected)
30 At depot
31 At destination depot
32 Depot checkout
40 In vehicle / on way to destination
50 POD / Delivered
51 ETA updated
52 Signature Updated
100 General escalation
101 Contact not available
102 Contact refused delivery
103 Wrong address
104 Delayed delivery
105 Item damaged
106 Directions required
107 Address changed
108 Failed collection
109 Lost
200 Collection escalation
1100 Arrived at destination country
1101 Released by customs
1102 Received at bonded warehouse

1.18.1.0 order.p2d_for_return.created Order created {}
1.18.4.100 order.p2d_for_return.status.atCollectionPoint Courier informed to collect for return {}
1.18.4.200 order.p2d_for_return.status.atCourierForReturn Parcel captured by courier for return {}
1.18.4.201 order.p2d_for_return.status.courierCollected Parcel captured by courier {}
1.18.4.202 order.p2d_for_return.status.courierAtDepot Parcel being processed by courier {}
1.18.4.203 order.p2d_for_return.status.courierInVehicle Parcel on route {}
1.18.4.204 order.p2d_for_return.status.courierPOD Proof of delivery provided by courier {}
1.18.4.300 order.p2d_for_return.status.completed Parcel returned {}
1.18.4.10000 order.p2d_for_return.status.cancelled Order has been cancelled {}
1.18.4.10010 order.p2d_for_return.status.notCompleted Parcel process p2d not collected closed {}
1.18.4.10030 order.p2d_for_return.status.courierEscalation Escalation. Pargo notified to resolve {}
1.18.4.10040 order.p2d_for_return.status.courierLost Parcel is lost {}

If you'd like to integrate our webhook service, please provide us with an endpoint to which we can direct all webhook requests.

Each webhook request follows a consistent structure, containing two main attributes at the root level:

  • data: This attribute contains the primary payload.

  • meta: This attribute includes metadata for the request.

Within the meta attribute, there is a unique webhookRequestReference identifier sent with each request. If you encounter any issues, please share this reference with us, and we will be able to locate and review the specific request to troubleshoot effectively.

The data attribute has the following structure:

Key Description
event A dot seperated, string based, reference for this specific event
eventCode A dot seperated, int based, reference for this specific event
description A human readable description of the event
timestamp A ISO8601 formatted date when this event has taken place
reference1 The reference used for the target of this event (eg. tracking code for an order or pickup point code for pickup points)
reference2 Optional second reference used for the target of this event (eg. for orders your external reference)
reference3 Optional third reference
extraReference1 Optional extra reference. Contents can be discussed with Pargo
extraReference2 Optional extra reference. Contents can be discussed with Pargo
extraReference3 Optional extra reference. Contents can be discussed with Pargo
payload Optional extra info regarding this event as a struct

Please contact Pargo regarding the available events and corresponding event references and eventCodes.

Example request:

{
    "meta": {
        "webhookRequestReference": "a7a9c891-3cbc-4e86-a0a7-af3bdb5a0917"
    },
    "data": {
        "event": "order.w2p.status.courier_captured",
        "eventCode": "1.1.3.4",
        "description": "Order PAR123123123 is captured by the courier",
        "timestamp": "2019-01-01T09:00:00+00:00",
        "reference1": "PAR123123123",
        "reference2": "EXTERNALREFERENCE",
        "reference3": null,
        "extraReference1": "EXTRA REFERENCE 1",
        "extraReference2": null,
        "extraReference3": null,
        "payload": {
        }
    }
}

Our webhook service includes an automatic retry mechanism for cases where a request fails to reach your endpoint.

Each webhook request will make a total of seven attempts over a retry period of 96 hours (4 days), following this schedule:

Retry Attempt Delay After Previous Attempt
1 60 minutes
2 3 hours
3 8 hours
4 12 hours
5 24 hours
6 48 hours

If the request remains unsuccessful after the final attempt, it will be marked as a failed request.

item.description:
Url: https://api.staging.pargo.co.za/events/orders/{{SEARCH}}
You’ll need to replace the {{SEARCH}} part with the tracking code (waybill), courier reference or the encoded external reference (if provided) for the order you want to process.

The response is splitted into 5 parts. You can include the include GET param in your request if you only want the api to return one or several of these parts. This can be done by putting the name in the include param separated with a comma (,). So if you only want the orderData and events you can add ?include=orderData,events to the url.

Key Description
orderData orderData struct
events list of event struct
currentStatus currentStatus struct
addresses list of address struct
steps list of step struct

OrderData

This will include some data about the order.

orderData struct

Key Description
trackingCode Tracking Code or Waybill
externalReference External Reference (if provided) or null
orderType Order Type (see Orders Module)

Events

This will include a list of events that happened for this order. There are major events and minor events. Major events are when an order goes from one stage to another. Minor events are smaller steps of progression until the next stage has been reached. Not every order will have every minor event.

event struct

Key Description
level String, either "major" or "minor"
date Iso8601 formatted date string, when did this event happen
code Unique code for this event
title Event’s title
description Event’s description

CurrentStatus

This will include the current status of the order.

currentStatus struct

Key Description
name Status name
code Unique code for this status
title Status title
description Status description
updatedAt Iso8601 formatted date string, when the order progressed to this status

Addresses

This will include a list of addresses used in this order. The order of the addresses in this list is based on where the parcel is going.
If an address is a residential address (eg. for home/door deliveries), it will not include the address values (only “type”: “residential”). But if the customer’s email address is provided into the request (GET param email) and it matches the mail address of that customer it will include all the full address data.

address struct

Key Description
type Can either be pickupPoint, warehouse or residential
reference Pickup Point's or Warehouse's reference
address1 Address line 1
address2 Address line 2
businessName Business Name
city City
country ISO 3166-1 format with 2 letters (eg. ZA, SZ)
postalCode Postal Code
suburb Suburb
province Province

Steps

This will include a list of steps that this order will go through to process it to the end.

step struct

Key Description
name Name
code Unique code for this status
active Boolean, indicating if the order has processed this step or not

Use the events module to get the current status of your orders. You’ll receive the current status, past events and even which future steps are going to be processed.

Note that there is no tracking information available for the orders in 'pending' or 'cancelled' status, and the request will return 422 statusCode.

item.name: Powered By Pargo
Authentication
item.description:
Auth

Retrieving and using tokens

We use the OAuth2 protocol for authentication. As of right now we don’t have a need for the grant_type, client_id and client_secret part of the protocol. Therefore the only thing you need to send us for the retrieval of a token is the username and password.

When requesting a token through an Authentication or Refresh token request you receive an access_token, refresh_token and expires_in.

To authenticate your API requests you need to add an Authentication header to all requests as follows;

Key: 'Authorization"

Value: 'Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN'

(notice the space between Bearer and the token)

The access_token expires after some time. This is indicated in the expires_in. expires_in is the amount of seconds the token is valid after creating it (now it is set to 4200 seconds or 70 minutes). When an access_token is expired you can request a new one using the Authentication request, but you can also use the Refresh token request. If you choose the second option you need to send us the refresh_token you've received when requesting the access_token. If the refresh_token is still valid, you'll receive a new access_token and refresh_token. Right now the refresh_token expires in 10 days.

Using the sendAs property

When successfully retrieving the token and sending it with every request, you also need to specify from which pickup point the request comes from (except for the /me and /me/pickup_points endpoint). Your user has permission to send requests for 1 or more pickup points. Pargo can set this up on their side and will communicate which pickup points you can use.

To specify which pickup point is used for the request you can put it in the body under meta.sendAs. If you also want to specify from which device the request is coming from, you can optionally send this as well. This will look like this:

{
   "meta": {
       "sendAs": "examplepup1"
   },
   "data": {}
}

Get Order Data

This endpoint can be used to retrieve the order data for the pickup point. This doesn’t start a process or execute any actions.

Note: Ensure that all requests include the X-Send-As header, which specifies the pickup point code (pickupPointCode). The returned orders will be filtered based on their association with the provided pickup point.

item.name: Customer In - ALL IN ONE (with formData and scan - search)

item.description:
As of right now there are 2 flows that a order process can have

  1. The easy one: print flow. No other actions are needed
  2. If the order is a p2w return with a paperless/drop-off code flow enabled OR the supplier has the w2p.use_sticker_for_p2w_return turned on, it needs to receive a SingleScan with a valid tracking code value from the sticker rol and the processing of the new FormData action in which we want to receive the customers name, mail and phone number.

To start the process you have to provide a lookup object. This object can have one of these parameters:

dropOffCode = this matches the drop off code connected to the order process

trackingCode = this matches the tracking code or courier reference connected to the order process

search = when this property is given, the system will check if this value is either a tracking code or a drop off code. This can be useful if the integrating party just has 1 input field and cannot make the distinction when entering in the lookup value.

Possible minor actions

  • formData
  • singleScan

When processing a major action you will create/start a new process. A process consists of a few values:

  • processId (string/uuid): this is the unique identifier for this process that needs to be included in every request related to this process

  • attributes (object): optional extra data related to this process

  • success (boolean): indicates if the request has been processed successfully

  • processed (boolean): indicates if the process has been fully processed and is ready to be finished

  • finished (boolean): indicates if the process has been finished

  • canceled (boolean): indicates if the process has been canceled

  • actions (list/array): a list of minor action structs which needs to be processed before we can finish the major action / process

Lookup

To start each major action you need to provide us with the lookup property. The contents of this lookup differs for each major action. This can be related to the order you try to process or indicating which courier is currently performing this action.

Starting and processing a major action

To process any major action (courier in, courier out, customer in, customer out) a process with an id will be created. There are different ways to actually process this.

Start - actions - finish

This is the longest way to process the action, but also the most flexible and gives the user the best feedback on what to do. The flow is this:

1. Start the major action process (URL/action/MAJORACTION/start). Request body differs from major action to action

In response you’ll get the `processId`

{
    "meta": {
        "sendAs": "pup0"
    },
    "data": {
        "lookup": LOOKUPSTRUCT
    }
}

2. Using the `processId` process the minor actions

{
    "meta": {
        "processId": PROCESSID
    },
    "data": MINORACTIONSTRUCT
}

3. When done, finish the process (URL/action/MAJORACTION/finish). This will either result in a success, warnings or errors. If there are only warnings, it will not finish the process. If you however want to force it, you’re able to add the ?force param to the url to force finishing the process even if there are warnings (this will not work if there are errors).

{
    "meta": {
        "processId": PROCESSID
    }
}

All in one

This is the shortest way of processing an action. Instead of making multiple requests (start, minor actions, finish) you can do all this in one. The upside is that this is fast and easy, downside is that this doesn’t scale well if multiple minor actions need to be processed.

To make 1 request which will start & finish the process (if possible), the user can go directly to the finish endpoint (URL/action/MAJORACTION). The body will be the same as what we would normally need for the start process endpoint. It can also include finish specific data and even include some action data if the implementation already knows that this is going to be the case. Example:

{
    "meta": {
        "sendAs": "pup0"
    },
    "data": {
        "lookup": LOOKUPSTRUCT,
        "actions": [
            MINORACTIONSTRUCT1,
            MINORACTIONSTRUCT2
        ]
    }
}

The response would either be a success response if everything is handled correctly. If there is an error in the ‘start/lookup’ portion of the request, the api will respond with an error but don’t start a process. If there is an error in one of the actions (either from within the action data or there are actions configured that aren’t processed here), the api will return these errors and will start a process and immediately cancel it. The process id will be included in the response.

item.name: Minor Actions
Single Scan

request.description:
Example action struct, in the todo are the valid values specified (you only have to provide on of them):

{
    "type": "singleScan",
    "subType": "static",
    "description": "Scan the barcode on the parcel",
    "requiredLevel": 0,
    "processed": false,
    "scanned": null,
    "todo": [
        "PAR2087800091"
    ]
}

Example when the single scan is processed

{
    "type": "singleScan",
    "subType": "static",
    "description": "Scan the barcode on the parcel",
    "requiredLevel": 0,
    "processed": true,
    "scanned": "PAR2087800091",
    "todo": [
        "PAR2087800091"
    ]
}

Example action struct:

{
    "type": "singleScan",
    "subType": "open",
    "description": "Grab a sticker from the roll. Stick it over the original barcode (if possible) and scan the barcode on the sticker",
    "requiredLevel": 2,
    "processed": false,
    "scanned": null
}

Example when the single scan is processed

{
    "type": "singleScan",
    "subType": "open",
    "description": "Grab a sticker from the roll. Stick it over the original barcode (if possible) and scan the barcode on the sticker",
    "requiredLevel": 2,
    "processed": true,
    "scanned": "PAR0081700930"
}

item.description:
The single scan action is used when we need a single scanned value to be returned to us. There are two subTypes that relates to the validation of the sent value:

  1. Static: for this one we provide the valid values in the todo param. This action is usually used as a check to make sure that the correct parcel is grabbed
  2. Open: when the subtype is open, you’ll have to send the value to the api for it to be validated

item.name: Multi Scan
item.description:
For the multi scan the api provides a list of values it expects the user the scan in. Every entry has optional extra information that could help identify the correct parcels. This action expects all the items in todo to be scanned, however if the required level is 1, the user can override any warning when finishing the process by using the ?force param.

Action struct:

{
    "type": "multiScan",
    "subType": null,
    "description": "Please scan in all these parcels",
    "requiredLevel": 1,
    "processed": false,
    "todo": [{
            "extra": {
                "collectCode": null,
                "dropOffCode": null,
                "customerName": "John Doe",
                "storageNumber": "937"
            },
            "values": [
                "PAR2016407441"
            ]
        },
        {
            "extra": {
                "collectCode": null,
                "dropOffCode": null,
                "customerName": "Jane Doe",
                "storageNumber": "782"
            },
            "values": [
                "PAR2016448096"
            ]
        }
    ],
    "scanned": []
}

Action struct when one value has been scanned in:

{
    "type": "multiScan",
    "subType": null,
    "description": "Please scan in all these parcels",
    "requiredLevel": 1,
    "processed": false,
    "todo": [{
        "extra": {
            "collectCode": null,
            "dropOffCode": null,
            "customerName": "Mick Wijnands",
            "storageNumber": "782"
        },
        "values": [
            "PAR2016448096"
        ]
    }],
    "scanned": [{
        "extra": {
            "collectCode": null,
            "dropOffCode": null,
            "customerName": "Mick Wijnands",
            "storageNumber": "937"
        },
        "values": [
            "PAR2016407441"
        ]
    }]
}

It is also possible scan a value not in the todo list. If so it looks like this:

{
    "type": "multiScan",
    "subType": null,
    "description": "Please scan in all these parcels",
    "requiredLevel": 1,
    "processed": false,
    "todo": [{
        "extra": {
            "collectCode": null,
            "dropOffCode": null,
            "customerName": "Mick Wijnands",
            "storageNumber": "782"
        },
        "values": [
            "PAR2016448096"
        ]
    }],
    "scanned": [{
            "extra": {
                "collectCode": null,
                "dropOffCode": null,
                "customerName": "Mick Wijnands",
                "storageNumber": "937"
            },
            "values": [
                "PAR2016407441"
            ]
        },
        {
            "values": [
                "PAR1234567890"
            ],
            "notInTodo": true
        }
    ]
}

item.name: Signature
item.description:
For this action you need to send the api a BASE64 encoded image string of the customers signature

Action struct:

{
    "type": "signature",
    "subType": null,
    "description": "Signature from the customer",
    "requiredLevel": 0,
    "processed": false
}

item.name: Payment
item.description:
Action struct, the amount specifies the amount that needs to be paid. A payment can be processed in all kind of ways, but if you use this api to mark it as complete you have to send the exact same amount otherwise the request will fail. If the payment has already been processed, the processed value can already be set to true.

Action struct:

{
    "type": "payment",
    "subType": null,
    "description": "Payment required",
    "requiredLevel": 2,
    "processed": false,
    "amount": 23.35
}

item.name: Form Data
item.description:
The form data action is used when we need some data to be retrieved from the customer. It includes a fields param which contains a list of field structs that is needed.

A field struct has these params:

  • name (string): the name of the field. Needed in the request
  • label (string): a nicely formatted string that can be used as the label in any front end
  • value (string): the currently set value for this field. This can either be filled in or null. Sometimes when we already have some customer information it can be pre-populated and can be used to just double check the values by the customer
  • valueType (string): the expected type of the value (currently only string, but examples are mail, date, etc)
  • description (string): a small description of this field
  • validationRules (array): a list of validation rules (currently only 'required', but examples are regex, mail, numeric, etc)

Action struct:

{
    "type": "formData",
    "subType": null,
    "description": "We need some extra data",
    "requiredLevel": 2,
    "processed": false,
    "fields": [{
            "name": "firstName",
            "label": "First Name",
            "value": null,
            "valueType": "string",
            "description": "Customers first name",
            "validationRules": [
                "required"
            ]
        },
        {
            "name": "lastName",
            "label": "Last Name",
            "value": null,
            "valueType": "string",
            "description": "Customers last name",
            "validationRules": [
                "required"
            ]
        },
        {
            "name": "email",
            "label": "Email",
            "value": null,
            "valueType": "string",
            "description": "Customers email address",
            "validationRules": [
                "required"
            ]
        },
        {
            "name": "phoneNumber",
            "label": "Phone Number",
            "value": null,
            "valueType": "string",
            "description": "Customers phone number",
            "validationRules": [
                "required"
            ]
        }
    ]
}

Every process has a list of minor actions that have to be processed before we can finish the process on its own. If the minor action is required you can find it in the minor action object.

Every minor action struct consists of these values:

  • type (string): unique name indicating which minor action type it is
  • subType (string): an optional sub type that gives some insight on how this particular action is being processed
  • description (string): a small description explaining what needs to be done
  • requiredLevel (int): this value indicates if this action has to be processed or not before you can finish the major action (see Required Level for more information)
  • processed(boolean): indicates if this action has been fully processed
  • Any optional data related to the minor action (documented at each minor action)

Required Level

Some actions must be executed before we can finish the process. Some actions can be skipped, but we really want them to be executed and other actions are completely optional. For these 3 situations we have set different levels:

0 = optional (you can skip these actions freely if you want)
1 = warning (if you don’t process these actions, we will return warnings after you finish the process. Also we’ll log these in Simba)
2 = mandatory (if you don’t process these actions, we will not let you finish the process. Also we’ll log these in Simba)

There are two types of actions when using this api: major and minor actions.

Note: Ensure that all GET requests include the X-Send-As header, which specifies the pickup point code (pickupPointCode).

item.description:
Full examples are provided with successful and unsuccessful responses for various order process types for this approach 'Start - minor actions - finish'.

Refer to the Major Actions section to see example requests of 'All in one' approach.

Powered By Pargo

The Powered By Pargo API is used by Pargo clients wishing to integrate their store management activities with Pargo's systems.

If you wish to make use of this Module, please contact Pargo to obtain Powered By Pargo Credentials. Note that SIMBA Authentication credentials will not work with the Powered By Pargo API.

Pargo Point Api Documentation

The Pargo point api provides the possibility to integrate an in-store application (like a POS system) to the Pargo’s in store fulfillment system. This facilitates everything that needs to be done at the Pargo Point to successfully process and manage Pargo orders. These things include accepting and handing over parcels to customers and couriers. Having an insight into the orders connected to your Pargo point and in the future stock management and all other related functionalities.

General request information

For the majority of requests a requestReference property is sent back in the response. You can find this value in the meta property. When you encounter any problems, please provide us with this reference and we’ll be able to look up this specific request and see what went wrong.

All requests and responses are formatted as JSON. When sending us requests make sure that the following header is set:

Content-Type: application/json
